title: Translate partner-priority term set into agreed language
context: oncology-glossary-multilingual's partner (partner-001) has specified a priority term list and agreed target language. This task translates those priority terms into the agreed language, with full translation review and back-translation QA for all safety-load terms, building on translate-001 and license-002. All entries must pass license-002 CI before packaging for delivery.
objective: Translate the partner's priority term set into the agreed language, using the termbase and translation review checklist. Obtain qualified translation reviewer sign-off on each entry. Perform mandatory back-translation QA for all terms with safetyLoad other than none.
acceptanceCriteria: All partner-priority terms are translated into the agreed language in glossary/<lang>/<term-id>.yaml, preserving meaning, negations, urgency, and preserved terms from the termbase
Qualified translation reviewer sign-off is recorded per entry; reviewer is independent of the drafting step and COI-declared
Back-translation QA is performed for every term with safetyLoad other than none, compared to the reviewed source definition before sign-off
All agent uncertainty flags are resolved or accepted-as-is with reasoning before sign-off
license-002 passes for every entry (attribution, provenance, notMedicalAdvice:true, license-compatible)
Entries are ready for packaging in delivery-001
resources: translate-001 output (prior translations and QA precedents)
output: glossary/<lang>/<term-id>.yaml for all partner-priority terms, with translation review sign-offs and back-translation QA results
